description: Relay is an agentic issue pipeline that moves issues through four stages — Preflight → Implement → Verify → Release — each handled by a focused agent with a bounded tool set. Human-in-the-loop is configurable at workspace, stage, and issue scopes, plus escalation rules that fire per stage transition. Relay ships as a local-first native app built on Laravel 13 / PHP 8.4 with NativePHP for desktop (macOS/Windows/Linux) and mobile (iOS/Android). A local Laravel server assists with OAuth callbacks, provider API calls, and subprocess orchestration; no cloud backend is required. Issues are pulled from GitHub Issues and Jira, implemented against local repositories using git worktrees (with configurable setup/teardown/run scripts), and released as GitHub pull requests. LLM calls route through the Laravel AI SDK with pluggable providers: Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, and Claude Code (invoked as a CLI subprocess).

userStories.title: Laravel 13 + NativePHP desktop scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Laravel 13 / PHP 8.4 project preconfigured with NativePHP so that the app runs as a local desktop binary from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: Laravel 13 project created with PHP 8.4 as minimum version in composer.json
NativePHP installed and configured; php artisan native:serve launches a desktop window
App bundles produced via native:build for macOS, Windows, Linux
Local SQLite database provisioned on first launch via migrations
Project passes composer install and php artisan test with a placeholder test

userStories.title: Core database schema
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want persistent models for the pipeline's core entities so that state survives app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Migrations for: sources, repositories, issues, runs, stages, stage_events, autonomy_configs, escalation_rules, filter_rules, provider_configs, oauth_tokens
Eloquent models with relationships (Source → Issues, Issue → Runs → Stages → Events)
Enum-typed columns for stage name, autonomy level, stuck state, source type
Seeder for default autonomy config (global: Supervised)
Factories for each model to support tests

userStories.title: Local OAuth callback server + secret storage
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to complete OAuth flows locally so that I can connect GitHub and Jira without routing credentials through a third-party server.
acceptanceCriteria: Dedicated /oauth/callback/{provider} routes handle redirect URIs
OAuth state parameter generated and validated to prevent CSRF
Tokens encrypted at rest using Laravel's encrypter with a per-install key
OauthToken model stores access token, refresh token, expiry, scopes
Token refresh helper invoked automatically when a request returns 401
Keychain/credential-manager integration via NativePHP secure storage for the master key

userStories.title: AI provider adapter layer
userStories.description: As the agent runtime, I want a single interface for LLM calls so that any provider can be swapped per stage or per workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: AiProvider contract defining chat(messages, tools, options) and stream(...) methods
Laravel AI SDK integrated as the base for HTTP-based providers
Implementations: AnthropicProvider, GeminiProvider, OpenAiProvider, ClaudeCodeCliProvider
ClaudeCodeCliProvider spawns claude via Symfony\Process; binary path configurable (default claude on PATH)
Per-workspace and per-stage provider selection persisted in provider_configs
Structured tool-call output normalized across providers
Unit tests with a recorded-response fixture for each provider

userStories.title: GitHub OAuth (custom flow)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect my GitHub account with repo, pull-request, and issue scopes so that Relay can read issues and open PRs on my behalf.
acceptanceCriteria: "Connect GitHub" button initiates OAuth with scopes: repo, read:org, workflow
App ID and secret configurable via settings (no hard-coded credentials)
Successful callback stores token and displays the connected account name
Disconnect action revokes token locally and calls GitHub's revoke endpoint
Error states rendered for denied consent and network failure

userStories.title: Jira OAuth 2.0 (3LO) custom flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect my Atlassian account so that Relay can read and update Jira issues.
acceptanceCriteria: OAuth 2.0 3LO flow with scopes: read:jira-work, write:jira-work, read:jira-user, offline_access
Accessible resources endpoint queried post-auth to list available Jira sites
User selects a site; cloud_id persisted per connection
Refresh token exchange implemented and exercised by the token refresh helper
Multiple Jira sites per install supported

userStories.title: GitHub API client
userStories.description: As the intake and release agents, I want a typed GitHub client so that issue fetching and PR creation are consistent and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Methods: listIssues, getIssue, listRepos, createBranch, pushBranch, createPullRequest, addComment
Rate-limit headers respected; automatic backoff on 403 rate-limited responses
Pagination handled for list endpoints
Requests attach the current token and refresh on 401
HTTP interactions mocked via Http::fake() in tests

userStories.title: Jira API client
userStories.description: As the intake agent, I want a typed Jira client so that issues can be fetched and transitioned.
acceptanceCriteria: Methods: searchIssues (JQL), getIssue, listProjects, listTransitions, transitionIssue, addComment
cloud_id scoped into every request URL
Labels, assignee, status mapped into Relay's Issue model
Pagination handled via startAt/maxResults
Tests mock Atlassian REST responses

userStories.title: Repository management + git worktree orchestration
userStories.description: As the implement agent, I want each run to execute in an isolated git worktree with configurable lifecycle scripts so that concurrent runs don't collide and per-repo setup is automated.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository model stores path, default branch, worktree root, and three script hooks: setup, teardown, run
Worktree created per run under the configured root (e.g., <root>/relay-<run_id>)
Setup script executed after worktree creation; teardown on run completion (success or failure)
Worktree removed via git worktree remove after teardown
Scripts receive environment variables: RELAY_RUN_ID, RELAY_ISSUE_ID, RELAY_BRANCH, RELAY_WORKTREE
Script output captured and attached to the run's event stream
Stale-worktree recovery on app startup

userStories.title: Sources management UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add, view, and remove connected issue sources so that I can control what feeds the pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: List view shows each source with type, connected account, last sync time, and status
Add-source action launches the relevant OAuth flow
Per-source "Test connection" button triggers a minimal API call
Remove-source action is confirmation-gated and revokes the token

userStories.title: Filter rules engine
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure per-source filters so that only relevant issues enter the queue.
acceptanceCriteria: Rules per source: include-labels, exclude-labels, unassigned-only toggle, auto-accept labels
Rules evaluated on every sync; matched issues created as Issue records
Auto-accept labels bypass the queue and enter preflight directly
Rule edits take effect on next sync cycle; no retroactive re-evaluation
Validation prevents conflicting include/exclude overlap

userStories.title: Issue sync workers
userStories.description: As a user, I want issues pulled from GitHub and Jira on a schedule so that the queue stays current.
acceptanceCriteria: Background job polls each source on a configurable interval (default 5 min)
Deduplicates by external ID + source; updates existing records on change
Manual "Sync now" action available per source
Sync errors surfaced in the source list with retry countdown
NativePHP scheduler wired to run queue workers locally

userStories.title: Incoming queue + accept/reject + pause intake
userStories.description: As a user, I want a queue of incoming issues where I can accept, reject, or pause intake so that I stay in control of what the pipeline works on.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue view lists incoming issues grouped by source with filter/search
Accept action transitions the issue to preflight
Reject action hides the issue and records the decision
Pause-intake toggle with configurable backlog threshold prevents new issues from entering
Auto-accepted issues visible with an "auto" badge

userStories.title: Autonomy levels + three-scope config
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set autonomy at global, stage, and issue scopes so that I can tune oversight without changing code.
acceptanceCriteria: Four levels: Manual, Supervised, Assisted, Autonomous — stored as enum
Scopes persisted: AutonomyConfig rows keyed by (scope, scope_id, stage|null)
Resolution function returns the effective level for (issue, stage)
Invariant enforced: stage overrides can only tighten from global; issue overrides can only loosen from stage
Validation rejects writes that violate the invariant with a clear message
Unit tests cover every scope-combination edge case

userStories.title: Escalation rules engine
userStories.description: As a user, I want escalation rules that always tighten autonomy when matched so that risky changes never run unattended.
acceptanceCriteria: EscalationRule model: condition (JSON), target level, scope
Conditions: file-path match, label match, estimated diff size, touched-directory match
Rules evaluated before every stage transition — not just at triage
Matching rule forces effective level to the rule's target regardless of other config
UI to add, edit, reorder, and disable rules
Matched rule recorded on the stage event for traceability

userStories.title: Orchestrator core
userStories.description: As the pipeline, I need a central orchestrator that drives stage transitions, enforces autonomy gates, and emits events so that agents run in the correct order with the right oversight.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator service manages stage lifecycle: start, pause, resume, bounce, complete, fail
Stage transitions evaluate autonomy + escalation rules and either auto-advance or pause for approval
Events emitted for every state change; persisted to stage_events
Stage execution dispatched to a queue so the UI stays responsive
Broadcast channel pushes live updates to the UI via Laravel Reverb or NativePHP events

userStories.title: Preflight agent — clarification mode
userStories.description: As the preflight agent, I want to front-load known facts and ask structured clarifying questions when the issue is ambiguous so that users can correct or confirm quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent decides "clear" vs "ambiguous" based on a confidence rubric
Known-facts panel rendered before any question
Questions rendered as radio-button choices where possible; free-text fallback
User answers persist on the run; agent resumes with answers in context
"Skip to doc" override available for impatient users

userStories.title: Preflight agent — preflight doc output
userStories.description: As the implement agent's upstream, I want the preflight agent to emit a structured doc so that downstream stages have a stable contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Doc sections produced: Summary, Requirements, Acceptance Criteria (numbered), Affected Files, Approach, Scope Assessment (size + risk flags + suggested autonomy)
Doc stored as Markdown on the run; rendered in the UI
Implement agent receives the doc — not the original issue — as its context
User can edit the doc before approving
Re-runs produce a new doc version; history preserved

userStories.title: Implement agent
userStories.description: As the implement agent, I want scoped tools for file editing, shell execution, and linting so that I can produce a diff inside the run's worktree.
acceptanceCriteria: Tools exposed: read_file, write_file, list_files, run_shell, run_linter, git_status, git_diff
Tools operate only inside the run's worktree; escape attempts rejected
Shell command timeouts and output size caps enforced
Agent cannot run tests (Verify's tool), cannot push or create PRs (Release's tool)
Live diff rendered in the UI as the agent works

userStories.title: Verify agent
userStories.description: As the verify agent, I want to run tests, static analysis, and coverage diffs against the implement agent's output so that quality gates are enforced before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Tools exposed: run_tests, run_static_analysis, coverage_diff
Test runner configurable per repository (PHPUnit, Pest, Jest, etc.)
Verify agent cannot edit files
Structured failure report emitted when any gate fails: test name, assertion, file, line
Pass → advance to Release; fail → bounce back to Implement with the failure report

userStories.title: Verify → Implement bounce with failure context
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the verify failure report to travel back to implement as a patch target so that iteration doesn't waste the agent's context re-diagnosing.
acceptanceCriteria: Bounce creates a new stage event implement.iteration.n on the same run
Preflight doc is reused — not regenerated
Failure report prepended to implement's context on retry
Iteration count increments; surfaced as ↺ N badge on the issue
Iteration cap per workspace — configurable — triggers a stuck state when reached

userStories.title: Release agent
userStories.description: As the release agent, I want to commit, push, open a PR, and update the changelog so that approved changes ship cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Tools exposed: git_commit, git_push, create_pr, write_changelog, trigger_deploy
Commit message and PR body generated from the preflight doc and diff
Changelog entry appended to the configured file
Deploy hook optional; fires after PR merge if configured
Release agent cannot modify source code (only commits and pushes what Implement produced)

userStories.title: Stuck states + give-guidance flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want stuck issues to surface with resolution paths so that I can unblock the pipeline without starting over.
acceptanceCriteria: Four stuck states detected: iteration cap, timeout, agent uncertain, external blocker
Each state shows its primary action (guidance, restart, take over, fix environment)
"Give guidance" opens a text box; submitted guidance is prepended to the next agent's context
Preflight doc stays valid — only the active stage retries
Stuck issues float to the top of the activity feed with unread dots

userStories.title: Run history timeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want a full timeline per issue with iteration labels so that I can read the story of a run.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline breaks into labelled sections per iteration
Each event shows actor (agent name or user), stage, timestamp, payload
Bounces and their failure reports visible inline
Diffs from each implement iteration expandable
Link to the PR once released

userStories.title: Activity feed
userStories.description: As a user, I want a global feed of agent events and human actions so that I can audit pipeline health.
acceptanceCriteria: Combined stream: agent state changes, user approvals, guidance, overrides
"N stuck" chip in the topbar, persistent until cleared
Filters: by source, stage, autonomy level, actor
Clicking an event deep-links to the run history

userStories.title: Issue view — 3-panel layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a three-panel view — queue, active issue, approval panel — so that I can work one issue at a time with context.
acceptanceCriteria: Left: queue of accepted issues sorted by stage + iteration
Center: active issue with preflight doc, diff, test output
Right: approval panel with context-appropriate actions (approve, reject, give guidance)
Keyboard shortcuts for approve (A), reject (R), next issue (J/K)
Responsive: collapses to a single panel on narrow widths

userStories.title: Live pipeline progress view
userStories.description: As a user, I want a live view of the current stage so that I can watch work happen in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Stage indicator shows current stage with stage color
Live diff updates stream as the implement agent writes
Test runner output streams during Verify
Changelog and PR preview rendered during Release
Reconnects gracefully after app sleep

userStories.title: Configure autonomy screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated screen to configure autonomy at every scope so that setup is visible in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Global default selector with explanation of each level
Per-stage overrides with the tighten-only constraint enforced in UI
Escalation rules list with add/edit/reorder/disable actions
Iteration cap input with validation (min 1, max 20)
Preview panel showing the effective autonomy for a sample issue

userStories.title: NativePHP mobile build
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want Relay on iOS and Android so that I can approve stages and give guidance away from my desk.
acceptanceCriteria: NativePHP mobile target configured; builds produce installable iOS and Android packages
Mobile-adapted layouts for issue view, activity feed, approval panel
Background sync respects mobile power/network constraints
Push notifications for stuck states and approvals awaiting
OAuth flows work with the platform browser in-app

userStories.title: Test coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want meaningful test coverage so that regressions are caught before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for every service: orchestrator, autonomy resolver, escalation engine, provider adapters
Feature tests for every stage transition path including bounces
Integration tests against recorded fixtures for GitHub, Jira, and each AI provider
CI runs php artisan test with coverage reporting
Coverage threshold enforced (start at 70%, ratchet up)

userStories.title: Developer documentation
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want clear setup and architecture docs so that I can run Relay and extend it.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md covers install, first-run, desktop and mobile build commands
ARCHITECTURE.md documents the orchestrator, stage contracts, autonomy resolution, provider adapters
CONTRIBUTING.md covers branching, commit style, and how to add a new provider
Each agent's tool surface documented in docs/agents/
Diagram of the pipeline and scope-resolution logic included
